Actinic Keratoses.advice: Consult a dermatologist for evaluation. Use broad-spectrum sunscreen (SPF 50+) daily.

Basal Cell Carcinoma.advice: Seek immediate medical attention. This requires professional treatment.

Benign Keratosis.advice: Generally harmless but consult if it changes appearance.

Dermatofibroma.advice: Usually harmless. Monitor for changes in size or color.

Melanoma.advice: URGENT: Consult a dermatologist immediately! Early detection is critical.

Nevus (Mole).advice: Monitor for changes using ABCDE rule. Use sunscreen regularly.

Vascular Lesions.advice: Generally benign but consult doctor for proper diagnosis.

Eczema.advice: Keep skin moisturized. Avoid harsh soaps and hot water.
Eczema.urgency: Moderate - If symptoms persist beyond 2 weeks

Psoriasis.advice: Use medicated creams. Reduce stress. Moisturize regularly.

Acne Vulgaris.advice: Wash face twice daily. Use non-comedogenic products.

Rosacea.advice: Avoid triggers like spicy foods and alcohol. Use gentle products.

Ringworm (Tinea).advice: Apply antifungal cream. Keep area clean and dry.
Ringworm (Tinea).urgency: Moderate - Treat promptly to prevent spread

Vitiligo.advice: Protect affected areas from sun. Consult dermatologist.

Urticaria (Hives).advice: Identify triggers. Take antihistamines as needed.

Warts (HPV).advice: Try OTC wart treatments with salicylic acid.

Impetigo.advice: Keep area clean. Apply antibiotic ointment.

Cellulitis.advice: Seek medical attention promptly. Requires antibiotics.

Scabies.urgency: Moderate - Prompt treatment to prevent spread

Folliculitis.advice: Keep area clean and dry. Use antibacterial soap.

Seborrheic Dermatitis.advice: Use medicated shampoos. Manage stress levels.
